title: Chicken Stock

overview.description: A rich homemade stock made from a leftover roast chicken carcass, gently simmered to extract maximum flavour.

method: Break the chicken carcass into a few large pieces so it fits comfortably into a stock pot.
Add the onion, carrot, celery, bay leaves, peppercorns and parsley stalks.
Bring slowly to a gentle simmer.
Skim off any foam that rises during the first 15 minutes.
Simmer gently, uncovered, for 3 hours. Avoid boiling.
Allow the stock to cool quickly.
Refrigerate overnight if you wish to remove the fat.

learning.did-you-know: Starting with cold water slowly draws flavour and gelatin from the bones, producing a richer stock.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to extract flavour from leftover bones and create a versatile foundation for soups, sauces and stews.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One Sunday roast provides the foundation for many more meals during the following weeks. Homemade stock adds depth that shop-bought versions rarely match.
chapman-tips: Don't add salt. Season the final dish instead.
Leave the onion skin on—it gives the stock a beautiful golden colour.
Keep the stock at a gentle simmer. Boiling makes it cloudy.
Freeze in 250ml and 500ml portions so you always have the right amount available.
Alternatiely, pressure cook for 1 hour, or slow cook for 10 hours
notes.leftovers: Use anywhere stock is called for throughout the cookbook.
